title: Lesson 439
transcript: Thank you. Okay, we just talked about HVAC systems, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems. There's one more little bit of information that you need to know here for the exam, and that is the ideal temperature and humidity ranges that a HVAC system is designed to maintain in, say, a data center. And this is important, equipment has, just like people, we're only happy if we're operating in certain temperature and humidity ranges, right? If it's way too hot, way too cold, way too humid, way too dry, we're miserable. Same thing with our equipment, right? If it's too hot, our equipment can overheat. If it's too humid, condensation can form, water can form inside your servers. If it's not humid enough, you can get static electricity. So it's actually really important for the availability of systems to maintain proper temperature and humidity ranges. So what are those ranges? And actually, a quick bit of trivia behind this. There was an organization known as ASHRAE that came up with these. It still exists. It's an organization in the US. ASHRAE stands for the American Society of Heating and Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineers, something like that. You don't need to memorize that for the exam. But here's the fun bit of trivia. They came up with these ideal temperature ranges for like office buildings like 40 or 50 years ago or 60 years ago or something like that. And they based these temperatures on an average Caucasian male. They based on all of these ideal temperature ranges on what would be ideal for like a male, middle-aged Caucasian male. And that's why in most office buildings, everyone's miserable except for the one white dude or whatever, right? Because like women, for instance, it's well proven. Women often will have lower body temperatures. They prefer the heat to be a couple degrees higher. Different people have different ideal temperature ranges, but that's not what Asher came up with 50 or 60 years ago. So that's a fun bit of trivia there. All right, here's the ideal temperature and humidity ranges. And you need to memorize these for the exam. So the ideal temperature ranges are between 18 to 27 degrees Celsius. People and our machines are happiest within those temperature ranges. And remember those ranges. 18 to 27 degrees Celsius. Now, if you're an American and you're watching this, It's a multinational exam. And so if you ever see a question on ideal temperature ranges, you'll see the answers in both Celsius, the correct way of measuring temperature and freedom height. So if you prefer measuring temperature, the incorrect way in freedom height, Here is those numbers, 64.4 to 80.6. So just memorize whichever makes more sense to you. 18 to 27 degrees Celsius, which is of course the correct way of measuring temperature, or 64.4 to 80.6 freedom height. Yes, I'm saying freedom height, not Fahrenheit. It's like Freedom Fries. You can also remember those ranges if you're American. I'm Canadian. I feel it's a requirement as a Canadian to make a little bit of fun of the US, right? We're like the annoying little brother of the US. So there you go. Freedom, right? Okay. And then here's the ideal humidity ranges, 40 to 60% relative humidity. So remember those two numbers for the exam or four numbers, I guess it is.
